channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-26
item.description:
The open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. In the `scienceverse/metacheck` project, a new feature was added to flag p-values reported as exactly zero, and the OSF prereg extraction was rewritten for improved handling. Meanwhile, `giladfeldman/docpluck` saw updates to its tables, documentation, and tooling, including a feature to detect column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables. Notably, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` and `JamieCummins/regcheck` also made strides, with the former localizing its confirmation email and review page, and the latter improving local reporting and multistudy ingestion. These advancements demonstrate the community's commitment to enhancing research tools and highlight the connections between projects, such as the shared focus on improving data handling and reporting. Overall, the community is celebrating a productive period, with many projects pushing updates and new features to support better research outcomes.

44 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck, auto-zcurve

Most active:

docpluck17 commits
metacheck12 commits
regcheck12 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `dev` branch saw the addition of a feature to "flag p-values reported as exactly zero" through the merge of the `exact_p_zero_warning` branch. On the `dev` branch, the OSF prereg extraction was rewritten "to schema-driven dispatch", changing how this process is handled. The `main` branch had its home page text updated to point to the Metacheck Manual. The `main` branch also had its navbar modified, replacing the "Get Started/Reference/Articles" links with a single link to the Metacheck Manual.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 6937e9c [dev] Merge exact_p_zero_warning: flag p-values reported as exactly zero — Lakens
  • 3c854b3 [dev] Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The commit on June 26 fixed issues with tables, specifically addressing "DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI" and mentioning "ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified" as part of the v2.4.98 update. On June 25, a feature was added to tables, introducing "RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables" as part of the v2.4.98 foundation. Additionally, documentation was updated on June 25, with the addition of "2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons" to the iterate documentation, although it was noted as partial. Another fix was made on June 25, which added a "direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note" and incorporated "RC-T/RC-1 spec findings" to the tooling.

2 commit(s) today:

  • b6bd65e [main] fix(tables): v2.4.98 — DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI (ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

Work was done on the "staging" branch, where local reporting was improved, as stated in the commit message "improve local reporting". On the same "staging" branch, multistudy ingestion was also improved, according to the commit "improve multistudy ingestion". The "staging" branch was the sole focus of these changes, with no other branches mentioned. The changes specifically targeted local reporting and multistudy ingestion, with no other features or modules mentioned. These two commits comprise all the recent work, with the most recent commit addressing local reporting and the previous one focusing on multistudy ingestion.

2 commit(s) today:

  • fdfb805 [staging] improve local reporting — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronized it with the `escicheckapp` repository at version `f37b5a1`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.6`, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6)". This commit is associated with issue `#20`. No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The commit brought the `main` branch in line with the specified version of `effectcheck`. The `main` branch now reflects the changes present in `escicheckapp` at `f37b5a1`.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman
  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: auto-zcurve: activity update
item.description:
shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve

The `main` branch was worked on, where the validation note was revised and a citation was added, as stated in the commit message "Revise validation note and include citation". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The revision of the validation note and the inclusion of a citation are the specific changes made in this commit. The commit message does not mention any other changes or additions to the repository. Only one commit was made, which was to the `main` branch, and it involved revising and adding a citation to the validation note.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
